privileged_info_summary: The sourdough starter calculator task uses 8 rows of synthetic feeding log data with columns for date, time, starter weight (g), flour added (g), water added (g), room temperature (°C), and hours since last feed. The task requires students to implement 6 spreadsheet formulas and checks: (1) hydration percentage formula =(E/D)*100, (2) total weight formula =C+D+E summing to 150-180g, (3) readiness logic using IF/AND for hours 3-8 and weight ≥150g, (4) total flour SUM formula, (5) hydration values within 70-150% range (all test data = 100%), and (6) average hydration AVERAGE formula. Pass threshold is 75% (≥5 of 6 criteria). All temperature-based hours mappings (temp ≥24°C→5hr, ≥20°C→7hr, <20°C→10hr) and readiness criteria are correctly specified and verifiable. The data contains temps from 21-24°C and hours from 11-13 (all outside the 3-8 hour readiness window).
